select_when: People need durable pattern recognition and usable retrieval under pressure, not just isolated facts or one-off memorization.
New material must attach to prior knowledge through analogy, chunking, scaffolding, or a clearer underlying map.
A recommendation or process needs mental compression that helps people decide faster and communicate more clearly later.
The real bottleneck is weak structure-building rather than missing effort alone.
avoid_when: A familiar template is being used as a substitute for re-seeing the concrete reality in front of the team.
Too many frameworks are being piled up without internalizing their core purpose, creating framework soup instead of a usable map.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on chunking, analogy, scaffolding, and durable pattern recognition rather than on general knowledge-management rhetoric.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor persona-encoding or AI-profile sprawl because the raw source is already strongest on human schema formation and map quality.
open_questions: The source reaches into communication, teaching, and strategy, but the operational file stays anchored to building a usable map that supports later judgment.

select_when.note: The raw source is explicit that schema acquisition matters when learners need durable pattern recognition, connection to prior knowledge, and usable mental compression.

avoid_when.note: The source explicitly warns against law-of-the-instrument misuse, resistance to change, and schema substitution for reality.

input_type.note: The source is strongest on map-building and durable pattern formation; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The raw model builds stable maps, chunks, and analogical connections; the output type compresses that into a short machine-usable label.

reasoning_types.note: The model diagnoses structure gaps and organizes knowledge systems, but the taxonomy labels remain reviewed normalization.
